title: Secure the local instance and establish Studio

claims.statement: The current REST routes are mounted without authentication middleware and the WebSocket handler accepts subscribe and production mutation messages from every connection.
The current HTTP and WebSocket listeners omit a host argument, so Node binds beyond the local loopback interface by default.
Editor, Panel, and the OBS output are separate Vite roots with no authenticated Studio shell.
claims.evidence: package.json#workspaces and editor/src/main.ts and panel/src/main.ts and client/src/main.ts

claims.statement: A login screen alone cannot satisfy secure-by-default behavior while unauthenticated REST or WebSocket mutation paths remain reachable.
A single local Owner carrying owner, producer, and designer roles is sufficient for the first OSS security boundary while the role model remains explicit for later users.

The final Caddy path layout for serving Studio, Editor, Panel, and Output from one production origin is not represented in this repository yet; this change proves the local multi-port topology and documents the required credentials.

successCriteria.statement: A fresh instance exposes only health and authentication bootstrap endpoints until exactly one Owner is created, after which bootstrap is permanently closed.
successCriteria.verification: Server integration tests for setup status, owner creation, duplicate setup, and protected route denial.

successCriteria.statement: Owner credentials use a memory-hard password verifier and interactive access uses expiring, server-managed, HttpOnly, SameSite cookies with logout invalidation.
successCriteria.verification: Authentication service and HTTP cookie integration tests.

successCriteria.statement: State-changing browser requests are rejected when their Origin is absent from the configured Studio origin allowlist.
successCriteria.verification: Adversarial cross-origin request tests for setup, login, and protected mutations.

successCriteria.statement: Every existing REST API requires a Studio session and every WebSocket connection requires either a Studio session or a valid Output token; Output credentials can subscribe but cannot send mutation commands.
successCriteria.verification: REST authorization and WebSocket capability integration tests.

successCriteria.statement: The Owner can rotate a Show-independent bootstrap Output token that is returned once, stored only as a digest, and invalidates its predecessor.
successCriteria.verification: Output-token lifecycle and timing-safe verification tests.

successCriteria.statement: The server binds REST and WebSocket listeners to loopback by default and requires explicit environment configuration for LAN exposure.
successCriteria.verification: Configuration unit tests and startup configuration evidence.

successCriteria.statement: Authenticated users can create, list, open, and archive Shows without seeing collection or channel identifiers.
successCriteria.verification: Show storage/API tests and Studio Shows workflow tests.

successCriteria.statement: Studio provides guarded Setup, Login, Shows, Production, Scenes, Editor, Library, Security, and Integrations routes under one stable application shell.
successCriteria.verification: Studio router, auth-state, and responsive shell tests plus desktop and mobile visual inspection.

successCriteria.statement: Existing Editor and Panel remain functional as authenticated transitional modules inside Studio while direct anonymous access is redirected to Setup or Login.
successCriteria.verification: Browser workflow tests across Studio, Editor, Panel, REST, and WebSocket origins.

definitionOfDone.statement: CHG-0007 references ADR-0004 and SPEC-0001 and its final implemented record contains no blocking unknown.
Authentication, origin enforcement, REST authorization, Output token rotation, WebSocket capabilities, local binding, and Show persistence have automated tests.
Studio setup, login, Show management, guarded navigation, embedded modules, and security settings are exercised in browser tests at desktop and mobile sizes.
Documentation and environment examples describe Studio as the entry point and require a tokenized OBS URL.
The complete governance, quality, test, and build suites pass for the current manifest.
definitionOfDone.evidence: npm run governance:check and npm run check and npm run build

definitionOfDone.statement: The signed pull-request commit and GitHub observation are current for the final planHash and manifestHash.
